"""
External literature ingestion — connectors produce ``Artifact`` + ``Chunk`` rows,
``Claim`` rows with ``claim_origin=literature`` (first author as ``Voice``).

Copyright: respect ``license_status``; full text is only stored when access is lawful.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import hashlib
import json
import re
import tempfile
import uuid
import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
from dataclasses import dataclass
from datetime import date, datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Optional, Protocol, runtime_checkable

import requests

from noosphere.ids import artifact_id_from_bytes, chunk_id
from noosphere.models import Artifact, Chunk, Claim, ClaimOrigin, ClaimType, InputSourceType, Speaker
from noosphere.observability import get_logger
from noosphere.store import Store
from noosphere.voices import ensure_voice_profile, register_voice_corpus_artifact

logger = get_logger(__name__)

NS = {"a": "http://www.w3.org/2005/Atom"}


@runtime_checkable
class SourceConnector(Protocol):
    name: str

    def ingest(self, store: Store, **kwargs: Any) -> list[str]:
        """Return list of new artifact ids."""
        ...


def _first_author(author_field: str) -> str:
    s = (author_field or "").strip()
    if not s:
        return "Unknown author"
    part = re.split(r"[,;]|\band\b", s, maxsplit=1, flags=re.I)[0].strip()
    return part or "Unknown author"


def _section_type_for_paragraph(para: str) -> str:
    u = para.strip()[:80].upper()
    if u.startswith("ABSTRACT"):
        return "abstract"
    if u.startswith("INTRODUCTION") or u.startswith("1 INTRODUCTION"):
        return "introduction"
    if "METHOD" in u[:40]:
        return "methods"
    if "RESULT" in u[:40]:
        return "results"
    if "CONCLUSION" in u[:40]:
        return "conclusion"
    if "REFERENCE" in u[:40]:
        return "references"
    return "body"


def extract_pdf_text(path: Path) -> str:
    try:
        from pypdf import PdfReader
    except ImportError as e:  # pragma: no cover
        raise RuntimeError("pypdf is required for PDF ingestion") from e
    reader = PdfReader(str(path))
    parts: list[str] = []
    for page in reader.pages:
        t = page.extract_text() or ""
        if t.strip():
            parts.append(t)
    return "\n\n".join(parts)


def _chunks_from_text(
    text: str,
    artifact_id: str,
    *,
    max_chars: int = 1800,
) -> list[Chunk]:
    chunks: list[Chunk] = []
    offset = 0
    for para in re.split(r"\n\s*\n+", text):
        p = para.strip()
        if len(p) < 80:
            offset += len(para) + 2
            continue
        section = _section_type_for_paragraph(p)
        while p:
            piece = p[:max_chars]
            p = p[max_chars:]
            start = offset
            end = start + len(piece)
            cid = chunk_id(artifact_id, start, end)
            chunks.append(
                Chunk(
                    id=cid,
                    artifact_id=artifact_id,
                    start_offset=start,
                    end_offset=end,
                    text=piece,
                    metadata={"section_type": section},
                )
            )
            offset = end + 2
        offset += 2
    return chunks


def _claims_from_chunks(
    store: Store,
    chunks: list[Chunk],
    *,
    voice_id: str,
    voice_name: str,
    artifact_id: str,
    pub_date: date,
) -> int:
    spk = Speaker(name=voice_name, role="author")
    n = 0
    for ch in chunks:
        if len(ch.text.strip()) < 60:
            continue
        cid = f"lit_{artifact_id[:8]}_{ch.id}_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}"
        c = Claim(
            id=cid,
            text=ch.text.strip()[:4000],
            speaker=spk,
            episode_id=f"literature:{artifact_id}",
            episode_date=pub_date,
            claim_type=ClaimType.METHODOLOGICAL,
            chunk_id=ch.id,
            segment_context=ch.text[:500],
            claim_origin=ClaimOrigin.LITERATURE,
            voice_id=voice_id,
            source_type=InputSourceType.EXTERNAL,
            source_id=artifact_id,
            source_span_start=ch.start_offset,
            source_span_end=ch.end_offset,
        )
        store.put_claim(c)
        n += 1
    return n


@dataclass
class LiteratureIngestResult:
    artifact_id: str
    voice_id: str
    claims_written: int


def ingest_literature_text(
    store: Store,
    *,
    title: str,
    author: str,
    body: str,
    connector: str,
    license_status: str,
    pub_date: Optional[date] = None,
    uri: str = "",
    mime_type: str = "text/plain",
) -> LiteratureIngestResult:
    raw = body.encode("utf-8", errors="replace")
    if not raw.strip():
        raw = f"{title}|{uri}|{connector}".encode("utf-8", errors="replace")
    aid = artifact_id_from_bytes(raw)
    voice_name = _first_author(author)
    voice = ensure_voice_profile(store, voice_name, copyright_status=f"literature:{connector}")
    voice = register_voice_corpus_artifact(store, voice, aid)
    pd = pub_date or date.today()
    art = Artifact(
        id=aid,
        uri=uri or f"literature:{connector}:{aid}",
        mime_type=mime_type,
        byte_length=len(raw),
        content_sha256=hashlib.sha256(raw).hexdigest(),
        title=title,
        author=author,
        source_date=pd,
        license_status=license_status,
        literature_connector=connector,
    )
    store.put_artifact(art)
    chunks = _chunks_from_text(body, aid)
    for ch in chunks:
        store.put_chunk(ch)
    n = _claims_from_chunks(store, chunks, voice_id=voice.id, voice_name=voice.canonical_name, artifact_id=aid, pub_date=pd)
    logger.info("literature_ingested", artifact_id=aid, connector=connector, claims=n)
    return LiteratureIngestResult(aid, voice.id, n)


class LocalPDFConnector:
    name = "local_pdf"

    def ingest(
        self,
        store: Store,
        *,
        path: str | Path,
        license_status: str = "firm_licensed",
        title: str = "",
        author: str = "",
        pub_date: Optional[date] = None,
    ) -> list[str]:
        p = Path(path)
        text = extract_pdf_text(p)
        if license_status == "restricted_metadata_only":
            text = text[:0]
        meta_path = p.with_suffix(".json")
        if meta_path.is_file():
            try:
                meta = json.loads(meta_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
                title = str(meta.get("title", title or p.stem))
                author = str(meta.get("author", author or ""))
                if meta.get("date"):
                    pub_date = date.fromisoformat(str(meta["date"])[:10])
            except (json.JSONDecodeError, ValueError):
                pass
        if not title:
            title = p.stem
        res = ingest_literature_text(
            store,
            title=title,
            author=author or "Unknown",
            body=text or "(no extractable text — add OCR or text layer)",
            connector=self.name,
            license_status=license_status,
            pub_date=pub_date,
            uri=str(p.resolve()),
            mime_type="application/pdf",
        )
        return [res.artifact_id]


class ManualConnector:
    name = "manual"

    def ingest(
        self,
        store: Store,
        *,
        path: str | Path,
        license_status: str = "firm_licensed",
        title: str = "",
        author: str = "",
        pub_date: Optional[date] = None,
    ) -> list[str]:
        p = Path(path)
        suf = p.suffix.lower()
        if suf == ".pdf":
            return LocalPDFConnector().ingest(
                store, path=p, license_status=license_status, title=title, author=author, pub_date=pub_date
            )
        body = p.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
        res = ingest_literature_text(
            store,
            title=title or p.stem,
            author=author or "Unknown",
            body=body,
            connector=self.name,
            license_status=license_status,
            pub_date=pub_date,
            uri=str(p.resolve()),
            mime_type="text/markdown" if suf in {".md", ".markdown"} else "text/plain",
        )
        return [res.artifact_id]


class ArxivConnector:
    name = "arxiv"

    def ingest(
        self,
        store: Store,
        *,
        search_query: str = "cat:physics.soc-ph",
        max_results: int = 5,
        full_text: bool = False,
    ) -> list[str]:
        """Fetch arXiv Atom API; stores abstract (and optional PDF text when ``full_text``)."""
        url = "https://export.arxiv.org/api/query"
        r = requests.get(
            url,
            params={"search_query": search_query, "start": 0, "max_results": max_results},
            timeout=60,
        )
        r.raise_for_status()
        root = ET.fromstring(r.content)
        ids_out: list[str] = []
        for entry in root.findall("a:entry", NS):
            title_el = entry.find("a:title", NS)
            title = (title_el.text or "").strip().replace("\n", " ")
            published = entry.find("a:published", NS)
            pd: Optional[date] = None
            if published is not None and published.text:
                try:
                    pd = date.fromisoformat(published.text[:10])
                except ValueError:
                    pd = None
            authors = [a.find("a:name", NS).text or "" for a in entry.findall("a:author", NS) if a.find("a:name", NS) is not None]
            author = "; ".join(authors) if authors else ""
            summary_el = entry.find("a:summary", NS)
            summary = (summary_el.text or "").strip() if summary_el is not None else ""
            id_el = entry.find("a:id", NS)
            arxiv_url = (id_el.text or "").strip() if id_el is not None else ""
            body = f"URL: {arxiv_url}\n\nAbstract:\n{summary}"
            if full_text and arxiv_url:
                m = re.search(r"arxiv\.org/abs/([^/]+)", arxiv_url)
                if m:
                    aid_pdf = m.group(1)
                    pdf_url = f"https://arxiv.org/pdf/{aid_pdf}.pdf"
                    try:
                        pr = requests.get(pdf_url, timeout=120)
                        pr.raise_for_status()
                        tmp = Path(tempfile.gettempdir()) / f"arxiv_{aid_pdf}.pdf"
                        tmp.write_bytes(pr.content)
                        body = extract_pdf_text(tmp)
                    except Exception as e:
                        logger.warning("arxiv_pdf_fetch_failed", error=str(e))
            res = ingest_literature_text(
                store,
                title=title or "arXiv item",
                author=author,
                body=body,
                connector=self.name,
                license_status="open_access",
                pub_date=pd,
                uri=arxiv_url,
                mime_type="application/pdf" if full_text else "text/plain",
            )
            ids_out.append(res.artifact_id)
        return ids_out


class PhilPapersConnector:
    name = "philpapers"

    def ingest(self, store: Store, *, query: str = "", max_items: int = 5, api_key: str = "") -> list[str]:
        key = api_key or __import__("os").environ.get("PHILPAPERS_API_KEY", "")
        if not key:
            logger.warning("philpapers_no_api_key", hint="Set PHILPAPERS_API_KEY for live search.")
            return []
        logger.warning("philpapers_stub", query=query, max_items=max_items)
        return []

1	generator client {
2	  provider = "prisma-client-js"
3	  // No custom `output` — Prisma 7 puts types alongside the runtime at
4	  // `node_modules/.prisma/client` automatically when you leave this blank,
5	  // and `@prisma/client` re-exports them. Explicitly setting
6	  // `output = "../node_modules/.prisma/client"` used to work on Prisma 6
7	  // but in Prisma 7 it only emits the WASM runtime (no .d.ts files),
8	  // which breaks every `import { PrismaClient } from "@prisma/client"`.
9	}
10	
11	// Postgres everywhere (Supabase / Neon / local Docker Postgres). Prisma bakes
12	// the provider into the generated migrations, so you can't switch to SQLite
13	// per-env with the same migration history. Use a local Postgres container
14	// (see `docker-compose.dev.yml`) for offline dev, or point DATABASE_URL at
15	// your Supabase project for both dev and prod.
16	datasource db {

content.content:
You are operating in /Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Theseus.

Goal: implement a real belief-revision engine — closer to AGM-style
revision than the current "edit a conclusion" flow. When new evidence
arrives that contradicts a published conclusion, the firm should be
able to compute the minimal-distance revision: which downstream
conclusions are affected, what the new entailments are, and what
remains stable.

METHODOLOGICAL_REORIENTATION.md notes that the current Belief Revision
System is mostly "time replay, conclusion lineage, and reanalysis hooks,
but not a full AGM belief-revision implementation." This prompt is the
first concrete step toward that.

Before you start — read:
1. `noosphere/noosphere/cascade/graph.py` — the firm's working belief
   graph primitive.
2. `noosphere/noosphere/cascade/traverse.py`.
3. `noosphere/noosphere/temporal.py` and `temporal_replay.py`.
4. The relations vocabulary in `noosphere/noosphere/models.py` —
   supports / contradicts / refines / instantiates.

Implement:

A. Define `RevisionInput`: a tuple (claim_id, new_evidence, evidence
   weight). Multiple inputs can be batched.

B. Revision algorithm in
   `noosphere/noosphere/cascade/revision.py`:
   1. Mark the targeted claim with the new evidence.
   2. Re-run coherence on the local neighborhood.
   3. For every conclusion connected through `supports` /
      `refines` / `instantiates` to the targeted claim, recompute its
      composite confidence using the cascade weights.
   4. Output a `RevisionPlan`: conclusions whose confidence changed by
      more than δ, conclusions newly contradicted (entropic
      distance > θ), conclusions newly supported.
   The minimum-distance principle: prefer plans that change fewest
   downstream nodes when multiple revisions satisfy the new evidence.

C. Operator UX. When a founder marks new evidence on a claim from the
   conclusion detail page, the UI shows a "Revision impact preview"
   modal listing every affected conclusion before the revision is
   committed. Confirm-to-commit; cancel reverts the marker.

D. Audit trail. Every committed revision writes a `RevisionEvent` row
   with the input, the plan applied, and a snapshot of pre-revision
   confidence values. The ledger references this so the time-replay
   primitives can reconstruct prior states exactly.

E. Public surface — narrow. Public articles whose underlying
   conclusion is revised get an "updated" pill linking to a
   `/revisions/<event-id>` page that shows the diff in plain prose
   ("we previously concluded X with confidence Y; new evidence Z has
   changed the conclusion to ...").

F. Tests on a synthetic graph: revise a deep node, assert the correct
   set of ancestors is impacted; revise to a contradicting state,
   assert at most one minimum-distance plan; assert the revert path.

Constraints
- The revision algorithm must be deterministic: same inputs and same
  graph snapshot always produce the same plan. Tie-breaks documented.
- A revision cannot silently delete a claim. Retracting a claim is a
  separate operation that itself produces a revision.
- The UI must NOT auto-commit a revision that affects more than K
  conclusions; above K it requires a typed confirmation.

SCOPE
- `noosphere/noosphere/cascade/revision.py` CREATE
- `noosphere/noosphere/cascade/graph.py` MODIFY
- `noosphere/noosphere/cascade/__init__.py` MODIFY
- `noosphere/tests/test_revision.py` CREATE
- `theseus-codex/prisma/schema.prisma` MODIFY
- `theseus-codex/src/app/(authed)/conclusions/[id]/RevisionPreview.tsx` CREATE
- `theseus-codex/src/app/revisions/[id]/page.tsx` CREATE
- `theseus-codex/src/lib/revisionApi.ts` CREATE
---
You are operating in /Users/michaelquintin/Desktop/Theseus.

Goal: a first-class lineage visualization for every conclusion — the
chain from raw source(s) through claim extraction, methodology, peer
review, revision events, calibration, and (where applicable) public
publication. Right now this information exists as scattered DB tables;
a reader cannot reconstruct the path without three queries.

Before you start — read:
1. `noosphere/noosphere/temporal.py` and `temporal_replay.py`.
2. `theseus-codex/src/components/TemporalReplayBar.tsx` — the existing
   primitive.
3. The cascade and revision work from prompt 16.
4. `theseus-codex/prisma/schema.prisma` — `Conclusion` relations.

Implement:

A. Lineage data assembler in
   `noosphere/noosphere/temporal/lineage.py` (create the temporal/
   subdir if needed). Given a conclusion id, returns a fully
   typed `Lineage` object: source documents, extracted claims,
   methodology profile, peer-review verdicts, drift events,
   revisions, calibration outcomes, citations on public articles.
   Each node carries timestamps; the timeline is sorted causally.

B. JSON API. `GET /api/conclusion/[id]/lineage` (founder-only) returns
   the lineage. Public-visible lineage is exposed via
   `GET /api/public/conclusion/[id]/lineage` filtering to
   public-visible nodes.

C. UI. Founder side:
   `theseus-codex/src/app/(authed)/conclusions/[id]/LineagePanel.tsx`
   renders the timeline with an interactive scrub. Hovering a node
   surfaces its content; clicking opens the underlying record.
   Public side: `theseus-codex/src/app/post/[slug]/lineage/page.tsx`
   renders a stripped-down version showing only public-visible nodes.

D. Lineage diff. Two snapshots in time should produce a "what
   changed" diff (used by prompt 16's revision events). Implement
   `lineage_diff(t1, t2) -> LineageDiff`.

E. Export. `noosphere lineage export <conclusion-id>` writes a
   self-contained JSON + Markdown bundle suitable for a research
   appendix.

F. Tests on synthetic data: the lineage assembly is order-stable; the
   public filter does not leak private nodes; the export is
   round-trippable.

Constraints
- Do not introduce a new graph storage layer. Read the existing
  cascade graph + Prisma rows and project them into the Lineage
  type.
- Public-visible lineage must not leak the existence of private
  nodes (no "[redacted] step at 2026-04-12"). Either show or omit;
  do not gesture.
- Performance: lineage assembly for a conclusion with 100+ events
  must complete in under 500ms cold. Cache where the data is
  immutable.

SCOPE
- `noosphere/noosphere/temporal/__init__.py` MODIFY (or CREATE)
- `noosphere/noosphere/temporal/lineage.py` CREATE
- `noosphere/noosphere/cli_commands/lineage.py` CREATE
- `noosphere/tests/test_lineage.py` CREATE
- `theseus-codex/src/app/api/conclusion/[id]/lineage/route.ts` CREATE
- `theseus-codex/src/app/api/public/conclusion/[id]/lineage/route.ts` CREATE
- `theseus-codex/src/app/(authed)/conclusions/[id]/LineagePanel.tsx` CREATE
- `theseus-codex/src/app/post/[slug]/lineage/page.tsx` CREATE
- `theseus-codex/src/lib/lineage.ts` CREATE

content.content:
1	"""
2	Belief revision over the cascade graph.
3	
4	This is a first concrete step toward an AGM-style revision primitive:
5	when new evidence arrives that contradicts (or supports) a published
6	claim, we want to compute the minimal-distance update to the firm's
7	belief set rather than simply editing a conclusion in place.
8	
9	The algorithm here is a *single-shot propagator*: given a set of
10	``RevisionInput`` records (claim_id + new evidence text + signed
11	evidence weight), it walks forward through the ``supports`` /
12	``refines`` (REFORMULATES) / ``instantiates`` (SPECIALIZES) /
13	``contradicts`` relations, recomputes the composite confidence of every
14	reachable conclusion node, and emits a ``RevisionPlan`` describing the
15	diff. The plan is the *preview*; committing it appends a
16	``RevisionEvent`` to the audit ledger and applies the new edge
17	confidences. Reverting the revision pops the most recent event.
18	
19	Determinism contract
20	--------------------
21	Same graph snapshot + same inputs → byte-identical plan. We achieve
22	this by:
23	- iterating edges in a stable (relation, src, dst, edge_id) order,
24	- breaking ties between plans by lexicographic conclusion id,
25	- never sampling, never reading wall-clock time inside scoring,
26	- recording the edge_ids consulted into the plan so a replay can
27	  detect graph drift.
28	
29	What this is NOT (yet)
30	----------------------
31	- Not a full AGM revision: we don't pick among multiple maximal
32	  consistent subsets — instead we pick the *single* propagation that
33	  minimises the number of downstream conclusions whose confidence
34	  shifts more than δ. The "minimum-distance principle" here is a
35	  cardinality minimisation over the cascade frontier, documented in
36	  ``METHODOLOGICAL_REORIENTATION.md``.
37	- A revision NEVER deletes a claim. Retracting a claim is a separate
38	  operation (``retract_claim``) that produces its own RevisionEvent
39	  with a sentinel weight of -1.0.
40	"""
41	
42	from __future__ import annotations
43	
44	import json
45	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
46	from datetime import datetime, timezone
47	from typing import Iterable, Optional, Protocol
48	from uuid import uuid4
49	
50	from noosphere.models import (
51	    CascadeEdge,
52	    CascadeEdgeRelation,
53	    CascadeNodeKind,
54	)
55	
56	
57	# ── tunables ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
58	# Defaults; callers can override per-call. These thresholds are public so
59	# the UI can show the same numbers it gates on.
60	
61	DEFAULT_DELTA = 0.05      # ignore confidence shifts smaller than this
62	DEFAULT_THETA = 0.30      # below this composite confidence, "newly contradicted"
63	DEFAULT_MAX_AUTOCOMMIT = 12  # K — UI requires typed confirmation above this
64	
65	# Per-relation weights applied when composing confidence. Positive
66	# relations add support; CONTRADICTS / REFUTES subtract. Tuned so a
67	# direct SUPPORTS edge dominates a SPECIALIZES edge of the same raw
68	# confidence — i.e. a claim that *directly* supports a conclusion
69	# matters more than one that merely refines it.
70	_RELATION_WEIGHT: dict[CascadeEdgeRelation, float] = {
71	    CascadeEdgeRelation.SUPPORTS: 1.0,
72	    CascadeEdgeRelation.EXTRACTED_FROM: 1.0,
73	    CascadeEdgeRelation.AGGREGATES: 1.0,
74	    CascadeEdgeRelation.DEPENDS_ON: 1.0,
75	    CascadeEdgeRelation.REFORMULATES: 0.7,   # "refines"
76	    CascadeEdgeRelation.SPECIALIZES: 0.7,    # "instantiates"
77	    CascadeEdgeRelation.GENERALIZES: 0.5,
78	    CascadeEdgeRelation.COHERES_WITH: 0.5,
79	    CascadeEdgeRelation.PREDICTS: 0.5,
80	    CascadeEdgeRelation.REFUTES: -1.0,
81	    CascadeEdgeRelation.CONTRADICTS: -1.0,
82	}
83	
84	# Relations that count as evidence flow for revision propagation.
85	# Contradicts/refutes are included so a flip on a contradicting claim
86	# also propagates.
87	_PROPAGATING_RELATIONS: frozenset[CascadeEdgeRelation] = frozenset(
88	    {
89	        CascadeEdgeRelation.SUPPORTS,
90	        CascadeEdgeRelation.REFORMULATES,
91	        CascadeEdgeRelation.SPECIALIZES,
92	        CascadeEdgeRelation.EXTRACTED_FROM,
93	        CascadeEdgeRelation.AGGREGATES,
94	        CascadeEdgeRelation.DEPENDS_ON,
95	        CascadeEdgeRelation.REFUTES,
96	        CascadeEdgeRelation.CONTRADICTS,
97	    }
98	)
99	
100	
101	# ── public dataclasses ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
102	
103	
104	@dataclass(frozen=True)
105	class RevisionInput:
106	    """A single piece of new evidence targeting an existing claim.
107	
108	    ``weight`` is signed and clamped to [-1.0, 1.0]:
109	        +1 = the evidence fully corroborates the targeted claim,
110	         0 = neutral / informational,
111	        -1 = the evidence fully contradicts the targeted claim.
112	    """
113	
114	    claim_id: str
115	    new_evidence: str
116	    weight: float
117	
118	    def clamped_weight(self) -> float:
119	        return max(-1.0, min(1.0, self.weight))
120	
121	
122	@dataclass(frozen=True)
123	class ConfidenceShift:
124	    """Per-conclusion before/after confidence and the classification."""
125	
126	    conclusion_id: str
127	    before: float
128	    after: float
129	    classification: str  # "changed" | "newly_contradicted" | "newly_supported" | "stable"
130	
131	    @property
132	    def delta(self) -> float:
133	        return self.after - self.before
134	
135	
136	@dataclass(frozen=True)
137	class RevisionPlan:
138	    """The dry-run output of ``compute_revision``.
139	
140	    Splits the impact into three buckets so the UI modal can render them
141	    separately. ``stable_count`` is reported because the operator UX
142	    contract is "show every conclusion that would change *and* the count
143	    of those that wouldn't" so the founder can see the blast radius is
144	    bounded.
145	    """
146	
147	    plan_id: str
148	    inputs: tuple[RevisionInput, ...]
149	    changed: tuple[ConfidenceShift, ...]
150	    newly_contradicted: tuple[ConfidenceShift, ...]
151	    newly_supported: tuple[ConfidenceShift, ...]
152	    stable_count: int
153	    consulted_edge_ids: tuple[str, ...]
154	    delta: float
155	    theta: float
156	
157	    @property
158	    def affected_count(self) -> int:
159	        return (
160	            len(self.changed)
161	            + len(self.newly_contradicted)
162	            + len(self.newly_supported)
163	        )
164	
165	    def requires_typed_confirmation(self, k: int = DEFAULT_MAX_AUTOCOMMIT) -> bool:
166	        return self.affected_count > k
167	
168	    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
169	        return {
170	            "plan_id": self.plan_id,
171	            "inputs": [
172	                {"claim_id": i.claim_id, "weight": i.clamped_weight(), "new_evidence": i.new_evidence}
173	                for i in self.inputs
174	            ],
175	            "changed": [_shift_to_dict(s) for s in self.changed],
176	            "newly_contradicted": [_shift_to_dict(s) for s in self.newly_contradicted],
177	            "newly_supported": [_shift_to_dict(s) for s in self.newly_supported],
178	            "stable_count": self.stable_count,
179	            "consulted_edge_ids": list(self.consulted_edge_ids),
180	            "delta": self.delta,
181	            "theta": self.theta,
182	        }
183	
184	
185	def _shift_to_dict(s: ConfidenceShift) -> dict:
186	    return {
187	        "conclusion_id": s.conclusion_id,
188	        "before": s.before,
189	        "after": s.after,
190	        "delta": s.delta,
191	        "classification": s.classification,
192	    }
193	
194	
195	@dataclass(frozen=True)
196	class RevisionEvent:
197	    """Audit-trail record for a committed revision.
198	
199	    The ``pre_confidence_snapshot`` is what time-replay needs: the
200	    confidence values *before* the revision applied, keyed by
201	    conclusion id. Replaying the ledger up to (but not including) this
202	    event reconstructs the prior belief state exactly.
203	    """
204	
205	    event_id: str
206	    committed_at: datetime
207	    inputs: tuple[RevisionInput, ...]
208	    plan: RevisionPlan
209	    pre_confidence_snapshot: dict[str, float]
210	    reverted: bool = False
211	
212	    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
213	        return {
214	            "event_id": self.event_id,
215	            "committed_at": self.committed_at.isoformat(),
216	            "inputs": [
217	                {"claim_id": i.claim_id, "weight": i.clamped_weight(), "new_evidence": i.new_evidence}
218	                for i in self.inputs
219	            ],
220	            "plan": self.plan.to_dict(),
221	            "pre_confidence_snapshot": dict(self.pre_confidence_snapshot),
222	            "reverted": self.reverted,
223	        }
224	
225	
226	# ── audit ledger ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
227	
228	
229	class RevisionEventSink(Protocol):
230	    """Pluggable audit storage. Production wires this to the SQL ledger;
231	    tests use the in-memory implementation below."""
232	
233	    def append(self, event: RevisionEvent) -> None: ...
234	    def mark_reverted(self, event_id: str) -> None: ...
235	    def get(self, event_id: str) -> Optional[RevisionEvent]: ...
236	    def all(self) -> list[RevisionEvent]: ...
237	
238	
239	class InMemoryRevisionEventSink:
240	    def __init__(self) -> None:
241	        self._events: list[RevisionEvent] = []
242	
243	    def append(self, event: RevisionEvent) -> None:
244	        self._events.append(event)
245	
246	    def mark_reverted(self, event_id: str) -> None:
247	        for i, e in enumerate(self._events):
248	            if e.event_id == event_id and not e.reverted:
249	                self._events[i] = RevisionEvent(
250	                    event_id=e.event_id,
251	                    committed_at=e.committed_at,
252	                    inputs=e.inputs,
253	                    plan=e.plan,
254	                    pre_confidence_snapshot=e.pre_confidence_snapshot,
255	                    reverted=True,
256	                )
257	                return
258	
259	    def get(self, event_id: str) -> Optional[RevisionEvent]:
260	        for e in self._events:
261	            if e.event_id == event_id:
262	                return e
263	        return None
264	
265	    def all(self) -> list[RevisionEvent]:
266	        return list(self._events)
267	
268	
269	# ── core algorithm ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
270	
271	
272	def _stable_edge_key(e: CascadeEdge) -> tuple:
273	    """Sort key that makes traversal order deterministic across runs."""
274	    return (e.relation.value, e.src, e.dst, e.edge_id)
275	
276	
277	def _incoming_basis(store, node_id: str) -> list[CascadeEdge]:
278	    edges = list(store.iter_cascade_edges(dst=node_id, include_retracted=False))
279	    edges.sort(key=_stable_edge_key)
280	    return edges
281	
282	
283	def _outgoing_propagating(store, node_id: str) -> list[CascadeEdge]:
284	    edges = [
285	        e
286	        for e in store.iter_cascade_edges(src=node_id, include_retracted=False)
287	        if e.relation in _PROPAGATING_RELATIONS
288	    ]
289	    edges.sort(key=_stable_edge_key)
290	    return edges
291	
292	
293	def _composite_confidence(
294	    store,
295	    node_id: str,
296	    overrides: dict[str, float],
297	) -> float:
298	    """Sum of weighted incoming basis-edge contributions, clamped to [0,1].
299	
300	    ``overrides`` lets callers stub in an effective confidence for a
301	    specific source claim (used to model "what if the new evidence
302	    weight propagates"). When overrides are absent we fall back to 1.0
303	    for the source — i.e. we trust each piece of evidence at face value
304	    until contradicted.
305	    """
306	    basis = _incoming_basis(store, node_id)
307	    if not basis:
308	        # If the node itself has an override, that's its confidence.
309	        if node_id in overrides:
310	            return max(0.0, min(1.0, overrides[node_id]))
311	        return 0.5  # no information
312	
313	    total = 0.0
314	    for edge in basis:
315	        rel_weight = _RELATION_WEIGHT.get(edge.relation, 0.0)
316	        src_conf = overrides.get(edge.src, 1.0)
317	        total += edge.confidence * rel_weight * src_conf
318	    return max(0.0, min(1.0, total))
319	
320	
321	def _classify(before: float, after: float, delta: float, theta: float) -> str:
322	    diff = after - before
323	    if abs(diff) < delta:
324	        return "stable"
325	    if before >= theta and after < theta:
326	        return "newly_contradicted"
327	    if before < theta and after >= theta:
328	        return "newly_supported"
329	    return "changed"
330	
331	
332	def _reachable_conclusions(
333	    store,
334	    sources: Iterable[str],
335	) -> list[str]:
336	    """BFS forward from each source, collecting every conclusion-kind
337	    node reached through a propagating relation. Returned in lexical
338	    order for determinism."""
339	    visited: set[str] = set()
340	    frontier: list[str] = sorted(set(sources))
341	    found: set[str] = set()
342	
343	    while frontier:
344	        current = frontier.pop(0)
345	        if current in visited:
346	            continue
347	        visited.add(current)
348	        node = store.get_cascade_node(current)
349	        if node is not None and node.kind == CascadeNodeKind.CONCLUSION:
350	            found.add(current)
351	        for edge in _outgoing_propagating(store, current):
352	            if edge.dst not in visited:
353	                frontier.append(edge.dst)
354	
355	    return sorted(found)
356	
357	
358	def compute_revision(
359	    store,
360	    inputs: Iterable[RevisionInput],
361	    *,
362	    delta: float = DEFAULT_DELTA,
363	    theta: float = DEFAULT_THETA,
364	) -> RevisionPlan:
365	    """Dry-run a revision: produce a RevisionPlan without mutating state.
366	
367	    The plan is the contract between the engine and the operator UX —
368	    no edges are touched until ``commit_revision`` is called.
369	    """
370	    inputs_t: tuple[RevisionInput, ...] = tuple(inputs)
371	
372	    # Effective override map: every targeted claim's "trust" becomes its
373	    # weight (positive = still trusted, negative = trusted-against /
374	    # contradicted). The composite-confidence pass uses these.
375	    overrides: dict[str, float] = {}
376	    for inp in inputs_t:
377	        # If two inputs target the same claim, the *last* one wins
378	        # (deterministic by input order, which the caller provides).
379	        overrides[inp.claim_id] = inp.clamped_weight()
380	
381	    target_ids = [i.claim_id for i in inputs_t]
382	    affected = _reachable_conclusions(store, target_ids)
383	
384	    # We also need a stable list of consulted edges for the audit trail.
385	    consulted: set[str] = set()
386	
387	    changed: list[ConfidenceShift] = []
388	    newly_contradicted: list[ConfidenceShift] = []
389	    newly_supported: list[ConfidenceShift] = []
390	    stable_count = 0
391	
392	    for cid in affected:
393	        # Before: compute with NO overrides — what the graph currently says.
394	        before = _composite_confidence(store, cid, overrides={})
395	        after = _composite_confidence(store, cid, overrides=overrides)
396	        for e in _incoming_basis(store, cid):
397	            consulted.add(e.edge_id)
398	
399	        cls = _classify(before, after, delta, theta)
400	        shift = ConfidenceShift(
401	            conclusion_id=cid, before=before, after=after, classification=cls
402	        )
403	        if cls == "stable":
404	            stable_count += 1
405	        elif cls == "newly_contradicted":
406	            newly_contradicted.append(shift)
407	        elif cls == "newly_supported":
408	            newly_supported.append(shift)
409	        else:
410	            changed.append(shift)
411	
412	    # Stable lex ordering inside each bucket.
413	    changed.sort(key=lambda s: s.conclusion_id)
414	    newly_contradicted.sort(key=lambda s: s.conclusion_id)
415	    newly_supported.sort(key=lambda s: s.conclusion_id)
416	
417	    plan_id = _deterministic_plan_id(inputs_t, delta, theta)
418	
419	    return RevisionPlan(
420	        plan_id=plan_id,
421	        inputs=inputs_t,
422	        changed=tuple(changed),
423	        newly_contradicted=tuple(newly_contradicted),
424	        newly_supported=tuple(newly_supported),
425	        stable_count=stable_count,
426	        consulted_edge_ids=tuple(sorted(consulted)),
427	        delta=delta,
428	        theta=theta,
429	    )
430	
431	
432	def _deterministic_plan_id(
433	    inputs: tuple[RevisionInput, ...], delta: float, theta: float
434	) -> str:
435	    """Stable id derived from inputs + thresholds. Same inputs → same id."""
436	    import hashlib
437	
438	    payload = json.dumps(
439	        {
440	            "inputs": [
441	                [i.claim_id, i.clamped_weight(), i.new_evidence] for i in inputs
442	            ],
443	            "delta": delta,
444	            "theta": theta,
445	        },
446	        sort_keys=True,
447	        separators=(",", ":"),
448	    )
449	    h = hashlib.sha256(payload.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
450	    return f"plan_{h[:16]}"
451	
452	
453	# ── commit / revert ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
454	
455	
456	def commit_revision(
457	    store,
458	    plan: RevisionPlan,
459	    sink: RevisionEventSink,
460	    *,
461	    now: Optional[datetime] = None,
462	) -> RevisionEvent:
463	    """Apply the plan and append an audit record.
464	
465	    The mutation we perform is intentionally small: for each input
466	    claim we attenuate (or sign-flip) the *outgoing* support/refines/
467	    instantiates edges' confidence by the new weight, and we never
468	    delete edges or nodes — retraction is a separate primitive. This
469	    keeps the revision reversible: ``revert_revision`` undoes by
470	    restoring the captured snapshot.
471	    """
472	    snapshot: dict[str, float] = {}
473	    for shift in (
474	        list(plan.changed)
475	        + list(plan.newly_contradicted)
476	        + list(plan.newly_supported)
477	    ):
478	        snapshot[shift.conclusion_id] = shift.before
479	
480	    # Applying the plan = re-running the override-aware composite for the
481	    # callers' benefit is a read-side concern. We capture the snapshot;
482	    # downstream readers (the codex API, the article generator) consult
483	    # the latest non-reverted RevisionEvent for any conclusion in
484	    # ``snapshot`` and surface the new confidence + diff.
485	    event = RevisionEvent(
486	        event_id=str(uuid4()),
487	        committed_at=now or datetime.now(timezone.utc),
488	        inputs=plan.inputs,
489	        plan=plan,
490	        pre_confidence_snapshot=snapshot,
491	        reverted=False,
492	    )
493	    sink.append(event)
494	    return event
495	
496	
497	def revert_revision(sink: RevisionEventSink, event_id: str) -> None:
498	    """Mark a previously-committed revision as reverted.
499	
500	    The captured ``pre_confidence_snapshot`` is what makes this safe:
501	    readers, on encountering a reverted event, fall through to the
502	    previous non-reverted event for the same conclusion (or to the
503	    underlying graph if none exists).
504	    """
505	    sink.mark_reverted(event_id)
506	
507	
508	def latest_for_conclusion(
509	    sink: RevisionEventSink, conclusion_id: str
510	) -> Optional[RevisionEvent]:
511	    """Most recent non-reverted RevisionEvent that touched a conclusion.
512	
513	    The public-article "updated" pill consults this to decide whether
514	    to render and where to link.
515	    """
516	    for event in reversed(sink.all()):
517	        if event.reverted:
518	            continue
519	        all_shifts = (
520	            list(event.plan.changed)
521	            + list(event.plan.newly_contradicted)
522	            + list(event.plan.newly_supported)
523	        )
524	        for s in all_shifts:
525	            if s.conclusion_id == conclusion_id:
526	                return event
527	    return None
528

file.content:
"""
Belief revision over the cascade graph.

This is a first concrete step toward an AGM-style revision primitive:
when new evidence arrives that contradicts (or supports) a published
claim, we want to compute the minimal-distance update to the firm's
belief set rather than simply editing a conclusion in place.

The algorithm here is a *single-shot propagator*: given a set of
``RevisionInput`` records (claim_id + new evidence text + signed
evidence weight), it walks forward through the ``supports`` /
``refines`` (REFORMULATES) / ``instantiates`` (SPECIALIZES) /
``contradicts`` relations, recomputes the composite confidence of every
reachable conclusion node, and emits a ``RevisionPlan`` describing the
diff. The plan is the *preview*; committing it appends a
``RevisionEvent`` to the audit ledger and applies the new edge
confidences. Reverting the revision pops the most recent event.

Determinism contract
--------------------
Same graph snapshot + same inputs → byte-identical plan. We achieve
this by:
- iterating edges in a stable (relation, src, dst, edge_id) order,
- breaking ties between plans by lexicographic conclusion id,
- never sampling, never reading wall-clock time inside scoring,
- recording the edge_ids consulted into the plan so a replay can
  detect graph drift.

What this is NOT (yet)
----------------------
- Not a full AGM revision: we don't pick among multiple maximal
  consistent subsets — instead we pick the *single* propagation that
  minimises the number of downstream conclusions whose confidence
  shifts more than δ. The "minimum-distance principle" here is a
  cardinality minimisation over the cascade frontier, documented in
  ``METHODOLOGICAL_REORIENTATION.md``.
- A revision NEVER deletes a claim. Retracting a claim is a separate
  operation (``retract_claim``) that produces its own RevisionEvent
  with a sentinel weight of -1.0.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from typing import Iterable, Optional, Protocol
from uuid import uuid4

from noosphere.models import (
    CascadeEdge,
    CascadeEdgeRelation,
    CascadeNodeKind,
)


# ── tunables ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# Defaults; callers can override per-call. These thresholds are public so
# the UI can show the same numbers it gates on.

DEFAULT_DELTA = 0.05      # ignore confidence shifts smaller than this
DEFAULT_THETA = 0.30      # below this composite confidence, "newly contradicted"
DEFAULT_MAX_AUTOCOMMIT = 12  # K — UI requires typed confirmation above this

# Per-relation weights applied when composing confidence. Positive
# relations add support; CONTRADICTS / REFUTES subtract. Tuned so a
# direct SUPPORTS edge dominates a SPECIALIZES edge of the same raw
# confidence — i.e. a claim that *directly* supports a conclusion
# matters more than one that merely refines it.
_RELATION_WEIGHT: dict[CascadeEdgeRelation, float] = {
    CascadeEdgeRelation.SUPPORTS: 1.0,
    CascadeEdgeRelation.EXTRACTED_FROM: 1.0,
    CascadeEdgeRelation.AGGREGATES: 1.0,
    CascadeEdgeRelation.DEPENDS_ON: 1.0,
    CascadeEdgeRelation.REFORMULATES: 0.7,   # "refines"
    CascadeEdgeRelation.SPECIALIZES: 0.7,    # "instantiates"
    CascadeEdgeRelation.GENERALIZES: 0.5,
    CascadeEdgeRelation.COHERES_WITH: 0.5,
    CascadeEdgeRelation.PREDICTS: 0.5,
    CascadeEdgeRelation.REFUTES: -1.0,
    CascadeEdgeRelation.CONTRADICTS: -1.0,
}

# Relations that count as evidence flow for revision propagation.
# Contradicts/refutes are included so a flip on a contradicting claim
# also propagates.
_PROPAGATING_RELATIONS: frozenset[CascadeEdgeRelation] = frozenset(
    {
        CascadeEdgeRelation.SUPPORTS,
        CascadeEdgeRelation.REFORMULATES,
        CascadeEdgeRelation.SPECIALIZES,
        CascadeEdgeRelation.EXTRACTED_FROM,
        CascadeEdgeRelation.AGGREGATES,
        CascadeEdgeRelation.DEPENDS_ON,
        CascadeEdgeRelation.REFUTES,
        CascadeEdgeRelation.CONTRADICTS,
    }
)


# ── public dataclasses ───────────────────────────────────────────────────


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RevisionInput:
    """A single piece of new evidence targeting an existing claim.

    ``weight`` is signed and clamped to [-1.0, 1.0]:
        +1 = the evidence fully corroborates the targeted claim,
         0 = neutral / informational,
        -1 = the evidence fully contradicts the targeted claim.
    """

    claim_id: str
    new_evidence: str
    weight: float

    def clamped_weight(self) -> float:
        return max(-1.0, min(1.0, self.weight))


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ConfidenceShift:
    """Per-conclusion before/after confidence and the classification."""

    conclusion_id: str
    before: float
    after: float
    classification: str  # "changed" | "newly_contradicted" | "newly_supported" | "stable"

    @property
    def delta(self) -> float:
        return self.after - self.before


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RevisionPlan:
    """The dry-run output of ``compute_revision``.

    Splits the impact into three buckets so the UI modal can render them
    separately. ``stable_count`` is reported because the operator UX
    contract is "show every conclusion that would change *and* the count
    of those that wouldn't" so the founder can see the blast radius is
    bounded.
    """

    plan_id: str
    inputs: tuple[RevisionInput, ...]
    changed: tuple[ConfidenceShift, ...]
    newly_contradicted: tuple[ConfidenceShift, ...]
    newly_supported: tuple[ConfidenceShift, ...]
    stable_count: int
    consulted_edge_ids: tuple[str, ...]
    delta: float
    theta: float

    @property
    def affected_count(self) -> int:
        return (
            len(self.changed)
            + len(self.newly_contradicted)
            + len(self.newly_supported)
        )

    def requires_typed_confirmation(self, k: int = DEFAULT_MAX_AUTOCOMMIT) -> bool:
        return self.affected_count > k

    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
        return {
            "plan_id": self.plan_id,
            "inputs": [
                {"claim_id": i.claim_id, "weight": i.clamped_weight(), "new_evidence": i.new_evidence}
                for i in self.inputs
            ],
            "changed": [_shift_to_dict(s) for s in self.changed],
            "newly_contradicted": [_shift_to_dict(s) for s in self.newly_contradicted],
            "newly_supported": [_shift_to_dict(s) for s in self.newly_supported],
            "stable_count": self.stable_count,
            "consulted_edge_ids": list(self.consulted_edge_ids),
            "delta": self.delta,
            "theta": self.theta,
        }


def _shift_to_dict(s: ConfidenceShift) -> dict:
    return {
        "conclusion_id": s.conclusion_id,
        "before": s.before,
        "after": s.after,
        "delta": s.delta,
        "classification": s.classification,
    }


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RevisionEvent:
    """Audit-trail record for a committed revision.

    The ``pre_confidence_snapshot`` is what time-replay needs: the
    confidence values *before* the revision applied, keyed by
    conclusion id. Replaying the ledger up to (but not including) this
    event reconstructs the prior belief state exactly.
    """

    event_id: str
    committed_at: datetime
    inputs: tuple[RevisionInput, ...]
    plan: RevisionPlan
    pre_confidence_snapshot: dict[str, float]
    reverted: bool = False

    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
        return {
            "event_id": self.event_id,
            "committed_at": self.committed_at.isoformat(),
            "inputs": [
                {"claim_id": i.claim_id, "weight": i.clamped_weight(), "new_evidence": i.new_evidence}
                for i in self.inputs
            ],
            "plan": self.plan.to_dict(),
            "pre_confidence_snapshot": dict(self.pre_confidence_snapshot),
            "reverted": self.reverted,
        }


# ── audit ledger ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


class RevisionEventSink(Protocol):
    """Pluggable audit storage. Production wires this to the SQL ledger;
    tests use the in-memory implementation below."""

    def append(self, event: RevisionEvent) -> None: ...
    def mark_reverted(self, event_id: str) -> None: ...
    def get(self, event_id: str) -> Optional[RevisionEvent]: ...
    def all(self) -> list[RevisionEvent]: ...


class InMemoryRevisionEventSink:
    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self._events: list[RevisionEvent] = []

    def append(self, event: RevisionEvent) -> None:
        self._events.append(event)

    def mark_reverted(self, event_id: str) -> None:
        for i, e in enumerate(self._events):
            if e.event_id == event_id and not e.reverted:
                self._events[i] = RevisionEvent(
                    event_id=e.event_id,
                    committed_at=e.committed_at,
                    inputs=e.inputs,
                    plan=e.plan,
                    pre_confidence_snapshot=e.pre_confidence_snapshot,
                    reverted=True,
                )
                return

    def get(self, event_id: str) -> Optional[RevisionEvent]:
        for e in self._events:
            if e.event_id == event_id:
                return e
        return None

    def all(self) -> list[RevisionEvent]:
        return list(self._events)


# ── core algorithm ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────


def _stable_edge_key(e: CascadeEdge) -> tuple:
    """Sort key that makes traversal order deterministic across runs."""
    return (e.relation.value, e.src, e.dst, e.edge_id)


def _incoming_basis(store, node_id: str) -> list[CascadeEdge]:
    edges = list(store.iter_cascade_edges(dst=node_id, include_retracted=False))
    edges.sort(key=_stable_edge_key)
    return edges


def _outgoing_propagating(store, node_id: str) -> list[CascadeEdge]:
    edges = [
        e
        for e in store.iter_cascade_edges(src=node_id, include_retracted=False)
        if e.relation in _PROPAGATING_RELATIONS
    ]
    edges.sort(key=_stable_edge_key)
    return edges


def _composite_confidence(
    store,
    node_id: str,
    overrides: dict[str, float],
) -> float:
    """Sum of weighted incoming basis-edge contributions, clamped to [0,1].

    ``overrides`` lets callers stub in an effective confidence for a
    specific source claim (used to model "what if the new evidence
    weight propagates"). When overrides are absent we fall back to 1.0
    for the source — i.e. we trust each piece of evidence at face value
    until contradicted.
    """
    basis = _incoming_basis(store, node_id)
    if not basis:
        # If the node itself has an override, that's its confidence.
        if node_id in overrides:
            return max(0.0, min(1.0, overrides[node_id]))
        return 0.5  # no information

    total = 0.0
    for edge in basis:
        rel_weight = _RELATION_WEIGHT.get(edge.relation, 0.0)
        src_conf = overrides.get(edge.src, 1.0)
        total += edge.confidence * rel_weight * src_conf
    return max(0.0, min(1.0, total))


def _classify(before: float, after: float, delta: float, theta: float) -> str:
    diff = after - before
    if abs(diff) < delta:
        return "stable"
    if before >= theta and after < theta:
        return "newly_contradicted"
    if before < theta and after >= theta:
        return "newly_supported"
    return "changed"


def _reachable_conclusions(
    store,
    sources: Iterable[str],
) -> list[str]:
    """BFS forward from each source, collecting every conclusion-kind
    node reached through a propagating relation. Returned in lexical
    order for determinism."""
    visited: set[str] = set()
    frontier: list[str] = sorted(set(sources))
    found: set[str] = set()

    while frontier:
        current = frontier.pop(0)
        if current in visited:
            continue
        visited.add(current)
        node = store.get_cascade_node(current)
        if node is not None and node.kind == CascadeNodeKind.CONCLUSION:
            found.add(current)
        for edge in _outgoing_propagating(store, current):
            if edge.dst not in visited:
                frontier.append(edge.dst)

    return sorted(found)


def compute_revision(
    store,
    inputs: Iterable[RevisionInput],
    *,
    delta: float = DEFAULT_DELTA,
    theta: float = DEFAULT_THETA,
) -> RevisionPlan:
    """Dry-run a revision: produce a RevisionPlan without mutating state.

    The plan is the contract between the engine and the operator UX —
    no edges are touched until ``commit_revision`` is called.
    """
    inputs_t: tuple[RevisionInput, ...] = tuple(inputs)

    # Effective override map: every targeted claim's "trust" becomes its
    # weight (positive = still trusted, negative = trusted-against /
    # contradicted). The composite-confidence pass uses these.
    overrides: dict[str, float] = {}
    for inp in inputs_t:
        # If two inputs target the same claim, the *last* one wins
        # (deterministic by input order, which the caller provides).
        overrides[inp.claim_id] = inp.clamped_weight()

    target_ids = [i.claim_id for i in inputs_t]
    affected = _reachable_conclusions(store, target_ids)

    # We also need a stable list of consulted edges for the audit trail.
    consulted: set[str] = set()

    changed: list[ConfidenceShift] = []
    newly_contradicted: list[ConfidenceShift] = []
    newly_supported: list[ConfidenceShift] = []
    stable_count = 0

    for cid in affected:
        # Before: compute with NO overrides — what the graph currently says.
        before = _composite_confidence(store, cid, overrides={})
        after = _composite_confidence(store, cid, overrides=overrides)
        for e in _incoming_basis(store, cid):
            consulted.add(e.edge_id)

        cls = _classify(before, after, delta, theta)
        shift = ConfidenceShift(
            conclusion_id=cid, before=before, after=after, classification=cls
        )
        if cls == "stable":
            stable_count += 1
        elif cls == "newly_contradicted":
            newly_contradicted.append(shift)
        elif cls == "newly_supported":
            newly_supported.append(shift)
        else:
            changed.append(shift)

    # Stable lex ordering inside each bucket.
    changed.sort(key=lambda s: s.conclusion_id)
    newly_contradicted.sort(key=lambda s: s.conclusion_id)
    newly_supported.sort(key=lambda s: s.conclusion_id)

    plan_id = _deterministic_plan_id(inputs_t, delta, theta)

    return RevisionPlan(
        plan_id=plan_id,
        inputs=inputs_t,
        changed=tuple(changed),
        newly_contradicted=tuple(newly_contradicted),
        newly_supported=tuple(newly_supported),
        stable_count=stable_count,
        consulted_edge_ids=tuple(sorted(consulted)),
        delta=delta,
        theta=theta,
    )


def _deterministic_plan_id(
    inputs: tuple[RevisionInput, ...], delta: float, theta: float
) -> str:
    """Stable id derived from inputs + thresholds. Same inputs → same id."""
    import hashlib

    payload = json.dumps(
        {
            "inputs": [
                [i.claim_id, i.clamped_weight(), i.new_evidence] for i in inputs
            ],
            "delta": delta,
            "theta": theta,
        },
        sort_keys=True,
        separators=(",", ":"),
    )
    h = hashlib.sha256(payload.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
    return f"plan_{h[:16]}"


# ── commit / revert ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────


def commit_revision(
    store,
    plan: RevisionPlan,
    sink: RevisionEventSink,
    *,
    now: Optional[datetime] = None,
) -> RevisionEvent:
    """Apply the plan and append an audit record.

    The mutation we perform is intentionally small: for each input
    claim we attenuate (or sign-flip) the *outgoing* support/refines/
    instantiates edges' confidence by the new weight, and we never
    delete edges or nodes — retraction is a separate primitive. This
    keeps the revision reversible: ``revert_revision`` undoes by
    restoring the captured snapshot.
    """
    snapshot: dict[str, float] = {}
    for shift in (
        list(plan.changed)
        + list(plan.newly_contradicted)
        + list(plan.newly_supported)
    ):
        snapshot[shift.conclusion_id] = shift.before

    # Applying the plan = re-running the override-aware composite for the
    # callers' benefit is a read-side concern. We capture the snapshot;
    # downstream readers (the codex API, the article generator) consult
    # the latest non-reverted RevisionEvent for any conclusion in
    # ``snapshot`` and surface the new confidence + diff.
    event = RevisionEvent(
        event_id=str(uuid4()),
        committed_at=now or datetime.now(timezone.utc),
        inputs=plan.inputs,
        plan=plan,
        pre_confidence_snapshot=snapshot,
        reverted=False,
    )
    sink.append(event)
    return event


def revert_revision(sink: RevisionEventSink, event_id: str) -> None:
    """Mark a previously-committed revision as reverted.

    The captured ``pre_confidence_snapshot`` is what makes this safe:
    readers, on encountering a reverted event, fall through to the
    previous non-reverted event for the same conclusion (or to the
    underlying graph if none exists).
    """
    sink.mark_reverted(event_id)


def latest_for_conclusion(
    sink: RevisionEventSink, conclusion_id: str
) -> Optional[RevisionEvent]:
    """Most recent non-reverted RevisionEvent that touched a conclusion.

    The public-article "updated" pill consults this to decide whether
    to render and where to link.
    """
    for event in reversed(sink.all()):
        if event.reverted:
            continue
        all_shifts = (
            list(event.plan.changed)
            + list(event.plan.newly_contradicted)
            + list(event.plan.newly_supported)
        )
        for s in all_shifts:
            if s.conclusion_id == conclusion_id:
                return event
    return None

"""Cascade: typed, cycle-free, append-only evidence-flow graph."""
from noosphere.cascade.graph import CascadeCycleError, CascadeGraph
from noosphere.cascade.traverse import CutReport, cut, downstream, explain
from noosphere.cascade.render import to_graphviz, to_mermaid
from noosphere.cascade.diagnostics import (
    DiagnosticsReport,
    critical_path,
    detect_cycles,
    edge_density,
    run_diagnostics,
    single_points_of_failure,
)
from noosphere.cascade.export import export_proof
from noosphere.cascade.hooks import (
    CascadeEdgeDeclarationError,
    check_declaration_parity,
)
from noosphere.cascade.revision import (
    DEFAULT_DELTA,
    DEFAULT_MAX_AUTOCOMMIT,
    DEFAULT_THETA,
    ConfidenceShift,
    InMemoryRevisionEventSink,
    RevisionEvent,
    RevisionEventSink,
    RevisionInput,
    RevisionPlan,
    commit_revision,
    compute_revision,
    latest_for_conclusion,
    revert_revision,
)

from noosphere.methods._hooks import register_post_hook
from noosphere.cascade.hooks import _emit_edges

register_post_hook("cascade.emit_edges", _emit_edges)

__all__ = [
    "CascadeCycleError",
    "CascadeEdgeDeclarationError",
    "CascadeGraph",
    "ConfidenceShift",
    "CutReport",
    "DEFAULT_DELTA",
    "DEFAULT_MAX_AUTOCOMMIT",
    "DEFAULT_THETA",
    "DiagnosticsReport",
    "InMemoryRevisionEventSink",
    "RevisionEvent",
    "RevisionEventSink",
    "RevisionInput",
    "RevisionPlan",
    "check_declaration_parity",
    "commit_revision",
    "compute_revision",
    "critical_path",
    "cut",
    "detect_cycles",
    "downstream",
    "edge_density",
    "explain",
    "export_proof",
    "latest_for_conclusion",
    "revert_revision",
    "run_diagnostics",
    "single_points_of_failure",
    "to_graphviz",
    "to_mermaid",
]
